skill.name: trade-show-budget-planner
skill.description: Plan trade show budgets, estimate exhibition costs, and calculate ROI for exhibiting, attending, or sponsoring events.
skill.summary: Create trade show cost plans with ROI scenarios, break-even math, and budget optimization guidance.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious behavior or prompt injection attempts. The scanner's command execution, path traversal, weak cryptography, and entropy alerts are false positives caused by markdown examples, relative documentation links, Chinese text, and budget wording. The only confirmed concern is low risk: the skill directs outputs to include Lensmor promotional links and may ask the assistant to search for public booth rates.

low_findings.title: External Promotional Link in Generated Outputs
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill includes hardcoded Lensmor URLs and instructs assistants to end every output with a Lensmor promotional footer. This is not malicious, but it can route users to an external site and adds marketing content to generated answers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly visible and the output-footer instruction confirms the intended external link placement. The link points to the author's trade show platform rather than showing credential theft or covert exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Optional Public Web Research
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill tells the assistant to search for actual booth rental rates when a specific show is named. This can cause network use in tools that support browsing, but the purpose is transparent public price research.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly requests searching for booth rental rates. No evidence found that the search sends secrets, local files, or private user data to a third party.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backtick alerts are markdown code fences around user prompts, installation documentation, and output templates. They do not execute shell commands inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation fences and markdown budget templates. No runtime script, shell invocation, subprocess call, or user-controlled command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The ../ paths are markdown links to related sibling skills in the README. They are not used for file reads, writes, imports, or filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear only inside markdown links under a Related Skills section. No code path consumes these strings for filesystem operations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to cryptographic functions or hash usage. The cited content is prose, table rows, or markdown output text for budgeting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain trade show budget descriptions, markdown table content, or promotional text. No cryptographic API, checksum validation, password hashing, or security-sensitive algorithm choice exists.

low_findings.title: Chinese README Entropy Alert Is Benign Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high entropy alert is caused by Chinese-language documentation in README.zh.md. No evidence found of binary payloads, encrypted content, obfuscated code, or hidden instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is readable localized documentation from the first line onward. The entropy heuristic is expected to over-trigger on non-Latin text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded External URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: Hardcoded Lensmor URLs appear in documentation, an example output, and the required output footer. This is a transparent external link pattern, not covert networking.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly present in reviewed files. No surrounding code performs automatic requests or sends local data.

content.user_title: Plan Trade Show Budgets and ROI
content.value_statement: Trade show costs are hard to justify when booth, travel, marketing, and staffing expenses are scattered. This skill creates structured budgets, ROI projections, and break-even views for exhibition decisions.
actual_capabilities: Builds itemized budgets for booth space, buildout, travel, marketing, staffing, logistics, and contingency.
Estimates ROI with conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios.
Calculates cost per lead, break-even deals, projected pipeline, and expected revenue.
Compares exhibitor, attendee-only, and sponsorship participation models.
Suggests budget optimizations based on goals, team size, show type, and experience level.
Formats results as markdown tables, CSV-ready summaries, or executive budget narratives.
limitations: Cost estimates depend on user inputs and may need confirmation from venues, contractors, and travel providers.
The skill does not guarantee actual booth rates, visitor counts, lead volume, or closed revenue.
ROI projections are planning models, not financial advice or revenue forecasts.
External market research may require browsing tools or current public sources.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Exhibitor Budget
use_cases.description: Estimate the full cost of taking a booth, including space, booth build, travel, marketing, staffing, and contingency.

use_cases.title: Justify Spend to Leadership
use_cases.description: Convert event costs into pipeline, break-even, cost-per-lead, and ROI scenarios for budget approval.

use_cases.title: Compare Participation Options
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether to exhibit, attend only, sponsor, or reduce scope before committing funds.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate a Simple Booth Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a trade show budget for exhibiting at [show name] with a [booth size] booth and [team size] staff. Use [currency] and list assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Attendee-Only Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an attendee-only budget for [number] people going to [show name] in [city]. Include travel, hotel, meals, registration, and staff time.

prompt_templates.title: Model ROI Scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Build conservative, base, and optimistic ROI scenarios for exhibiting at [show name]. Our average deal size is [amount], and our target customer is [audience].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Exhibit Strategies
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare three options for [show name]: a small shell booth, a custom booth, and attending with scheduled meetings. Include cost, expected leads, ROI, risks, and recommendation.

output_examples.input: How much would a 20 square meter booth at MEDICA cost for four people?
output_examples.output: A structured estimate with space, booth build, travel, marketing, operations, contingency, total budget, and notes on which assumptions need venue confirmation.

output_examples.input: Is a booth worth it if our average deal size is 50,000 dollars?
output_examples.output: An ROI model showing required leads, expected opportunities, projected deals, break-even point, cost per lead, and conservative versus optimistic outcomes.

output_examples.input: Help me justify next year's trade show plan to my boss.
output_examples.output: An executive summary that explains budget categories, expected pipeline value, non-financial benefits, risk controls, and practical cost reductions.

best_practices: Provide booth size, team size, city, travel origin, participation type, average deal size, and sales conversion assumptions.
Ask the skill to label confirmed costs separately from estimates and assumptions.
Request conservative scenarios first, then compare upside only after the break-even case is clear.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generic benchmark costs as final vendor quotes or venue invoices.
Do not omit staff time, shipping, insurance, internet, electrical, or contingency from the budget.
Do not use one optimistic lead estimate as the only ROI case for approval.

faq.question: Can this skill calculate a full exhibitor budget?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure booth, travel, marketing, staffing, logistics, and contingency costs into a planning budget.

faq.question: Can it estimate ROI?
faq.answer: Yes. It can model leads, opportunities, deals, revenue, cost per lead, and break-even deals.

faq.question: Does it know current booth rental rates?
faq.answer: It can use benchmarks and may search public sources when browsing is available. Venue quotes remain the best source.

faq.question: Can it compare attending versus exhibiting?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare costs, expected outcomes, risks, and recommended next steps for each option.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning estimates and decision support, not financial, accounting, or legal advice.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the show name, location, booth size, team size, goals, budget range, average deal size, and known conversion rates.
